- calibrate rover1 camera2 objective4 waypoint6 (precondition: equipped_for_imaging rover1, energy>=2, calibration_target camera2 objective4, in rover1 waypoint6, visible_from objective4 waypoint6, on_board camera2 rover1). All true. Energy 50->48.
- take_image rover1 waypoint6 objective1 camera2 high_res (precondition: calibrated camera2 rover1, on_board camera2 rover1, equipped_for_imaging rover1, supports camera2 high_res, visible_from objective1 waypoint6, in rover1 waypoint6, energy>=1). After calibrate, calibrated true. Energy 48->47. Effect: have_image rover1 objective1 high_res, not calibrated camera2 rover1.
- calibrate rover1 camera2 objective4 waypoint6 (again, energy 47->45)
- take_image rover1 waypoint6 objective1 camera2 low_res (energy 45->44)
- communicate_image_data rover1 general objective1 high_res waypoint6 waypoint9 (precondition: in rover1 waypoint6, at_lander general waypoint9, have_image rover1 objective1 high_res, visible waypoint6 waypoint9, available rover1, channel_free general, energy>=6). Energy 44->38.
- communicate_image_data rover1 general objective1 low_res waypoint6 waypoint9 (energy 38->32).

Now Rover1 done.

Next, Rover2 at waypoint6, energy 50.

Rover2: equipped_for_soil_analysis, store rover2store empty.

We need to do soil at waypoint5 and waypoint10.

First, waypoint5: navigate rover2 waypoint6 waypoint5 (can_traverse rover2 waypoint6 waypoint5? Yes, listed. visible waypoint6 waypoint5? visible waypoint5 waypoint6 and waypoint6 waypoint5 are both in visible list. So visible condition satisfied. Energy 50->42.
- sample_soil rover2 rover2store waypoint5 (at_soil_sample waypoint5 true, equipped_for_soil_analysis rover2, store_of rover2store rover2, empty rover2store, energy>=3). Energy 42->39. Store full, have_soil_analysis rover2 waypoint5.
- navigate rover2 waypoint5 waypoint6 (energy 39->31).
- communicate_soil_data rover2 general waypoint5 waypoint6 waypoint9 (precondition: in rover2 waypoint6, at_lander general waypoint9, have_soil_analysis rover2 waypoint5, visible waypoint6 waypoint9, available rover2, channel_free general, energy>=4). Energy 31->27. Effect: communicated_soil_data waypoint5.
- drop rover2 rover2store (store full -> empty).

Now waypoint10: navigate rover2 waypoint6 waypoint10 (can_traverse rover2 waypoint6 waypoint10, visible waypoint6 waypoint10? visible waypoint10 waypoint6 and waypoint6 waypoint10 both listed. Energy 27->19.
- sample_soil rover2 rover2store waypoint10 (at_soil_sample waypoint10 true, energy>=3). Energy 19->16.
- navigate rover2 waypoint10 waypoint6 (energy 16->8).
- communicate_soil_data rover2 general waypoint10 waypoint6 waypoint9 (energy 8->4).
- drop rover2 rover2store.

Rover2 done. Energy 4 left.

Now Rover0 at waypoint4, energy 50.

Rover0: equipped_for_soil_analysis, store rover0store empty.

We'll do waypoint8 first (since it's visible to lander, can communicate from there).

- navigate rover0 waypoint4 waypoint8 (can_traverse rover0 waypoint4 waypoint8, visible waypoint4 waypoint8? visible waypoint8 waypoint4 and waypoint4 waypoint8 both listed. Energy 50->42.
- sample_soil rover0 rover0store waypoint8 (at_soil_sample waypoint8 true, energy>=3). Energy 42->39.
- communicate_soil_data rover0 general waypoint8 waypoint8 waypoint9 (precondition: in rover0 waypoint8, at_lander general waypoint9, have_soil_analysis rover0 waypoint8, visible waypoint8 waypoint9, available rover0, channel_free general, energy>=4). Energy 39->35.
- drop rover0 rover0store.

Now at waypoint8, energy 35. Next, waypoint0. Need to go to waypoint0. From waypoint8, rover0 can traverse 8->4 (can_traverse rover0 waypoint8 waypoint4). Then 4->0. So:
- navigate rover0 waypoint8 waypoint4 (energy 35->27).
- navigate rover0 waypoint4 waypoint0 (energy 27->19).
- sample_soil rover0 rover0store waypoint0 (at_soil_sample waypoint0 true, energy>=3). Energy 19->16.
- Now need to communicate. Waypoint0 not visible to 9. Go to waypoint3 (visible to 9 and sunny). rover0 can traverse 0->3 (can_traverse rover0 waypoint0 waypoint3). visible waypoint0 waypoint3? visible waypoint3 waypoint0 and waypoint0 waypoint3 both listed. Energy 16->8.
- communicate_soil_data rover0 general waypoint0 waypoint3 waypoint9 (precondition: in rover0 waypoint3, at_lander general waypoint9, have_soil_analysis rover0 waypoint0, visible waypoint3 waypoint9, available rover0, channel_free general, energy>=4). Energy 8->4.
- drop rover0 rover0store.

Now at waypoint3, energy 4. Waypoint3 is sunny (in_sun waypoint3). We can recharge to get energy for next trips.
- recharge rover0 waypoint3 (precondition: in rover0 waypoint3, in_sun waypoint3, energy<=80). Energy 4->24. (recharges increased)

Now need to go to waypoint4 to then go to waypoint2. From waypoint3, rover0 can only traverse to waypoint0 (can_traverse rover0 waypoint3 waypoint0). So:
- navigate rover0 waypoint3 waypoint0 (energy 24->16).
- navigate rover0 waypoint0 waypoint4 (energy 16->8).
- Now at waypoint4, energy 8. Need to do waypoint2. navigate rover0 waypoint4 waypoint2 (energy 8->0). But sample_soil requires energy>=3. So we need more energy. At waypoint4 not sunny. Could we have recharged more at waypoint3? We recharged once to 24. Could recharge again at waypoint3 before leaving? But after recharge, energy 24, we navigated to 0 (8) ->16, to 4 (8) ->8. If we recharge twice at waypoint3: first recharge 4->24, second recharge 24->44 (since <=80). Then navigate 3->0 (8)->36, 0->4 (8)->28. Then navigate 4->2 (8)->20. sample_soil (3)->17. Then need to communicate. From waypoint2, not visible to 9. Options: go to waypoint4 (8)->9, then 4->9 (8)->1, communicate (4) -> -3 not enough. Or go to waypoint8? 2->4 (8)->9, 4->8 (8)->1, communicate from 8 (4) -> -3. Or go to waypoint9 via 4->9 (8) from 4: after sample at 2, energy 17, go 2->4 (8)->9, 4->9 (8)->1, communicate (4) -> -3. So need more energy. Could recharge at waypoint9 after arriving? But we need energy to get to 9. At 2 with 17, go to 4 (8)->9, at 4 with 9, go to 9 (8)->1, at 9 with 1, cannot communicate (need 4). Could recharge at 9? But energy 1 <=80, so we can recharge at 9 (sunny) to 21, then communicate (4)->17. That works! But we must be at 9 to recharge. So from 2, go to 4 (8), then 4->9 (8), arrive at 9 with 1 energy, recharge (20) ->21, communicate (4)->17, drop. Then we are done with soil samples.

But we also have waypoint5 and 10 already done by rover2. So rover0 only needs 0,2,8. We did 8,0,2. Good.

Let's adjust rover0 plan: after waypoint0 communicate at 3, recharge twice at 3 (or once more) to have enough energy for 2 and communication at 9.

Let's compute with two recharges at 3:
After communicate at 3, energy 4.
- recharge rover0 waypoint3 -> energy 24.
- recharge rover0 waypoint3 -> energy 44.
- navigate rover0 waypoint3 waypoint0 -> 36.
- navigate rover0 waypoint0 waypoint4 -> 28.
- navigate rover0 waypoint4 waypoint2 -> 20.
- sample_soil rover0 rover0store waypoint2 -> 17.
- navigate rover0 waypoint2 waypoint4 -> 9.
- navigate rover0 waypoint4 waypoint9 -> 1.
- recharge rover0 waypoint9 -> 21.
- communicate_soil_data rover0 general waypoint2 waypoint9 waypoint9 -> 17.
- drop rover0 rover0store.

Check preconditions: communicate_soil_data from waypoint9: in rover0 waypoint9, at_lander general waypoint9, have_soil_analysis rover0 waypoint2, visible waypoint9 waypoint9? visible waypoint9 waypoint9? Not explicitly listed. The visible predicate is symmetric? In the problem, visible is given for pairs. Is visible

messages.content:
You are a PDDL planning assistant.

Your task is to produce a valid plan for the given PDDL domain and problem.

Global rules:
- Use only action schemas explicitly defined in the domain.
- Never invent action names, predicates, objects, or numeric fluents.
- Never output predicates from the initial state or goal as if they were actions.
- Never output assignments, numeric conditions, goals, or intermediate states.
- A plan is a sequence of executable domain actions.
- Each action must be written as one parenthesized PDDL action per line.
- Use only object names that appear in the problem.
- Respect action preconditions, effects, numeric constraints, and the final goal.
- Use the lowest number of actions possible.

When the protocol requires plan-only output:
- Return only the final action sequence.
- Emit the complete final action sequence in the visible assistant content, not only in hidden reasoning or thinking content.
- Before finalizing, verify that the visible action sequence achieves every goal condition.
- Do not stop before emitting the action or actions that make the goal true.
- Do not append extra actions after the first goal-satisfying point.
- Do not include explanations, headings, markdown fences, bullets, numbering, or comments.

TASK FAMILY: rover
DIFFICULTY: hard
INSTANCE: pfile15

TASK FAMILY: rover

You are solving a PDDL planning problem with numeric rover energy.

The domain models rovers navigating between waypoints, collecting soil and rock
samples, taking images, and communicating data to a lander. Rovers consume
energy for navigation, sampling, calibration, imaging, and communication.

Use only the action schemas defined in the domain:
- navigate
- recharge
- sample_soil
- sample_rock
- drop
- calibrate
- take_image
- communicate_soil_data
- communicate_rock_data
- communicate_image_data

Action interpretation:
- navigate moves a rover between visible, traversable waypoints and costs 8
  energy.
- recharge increases rover energy by 20 at sunny waypoints when energy is at
  most 80.
- sample_soil and sample_rock require the correct equipment, a matching sample
  at the current waypoint, an empty store, and enough energy.
- drop empties a full store for later reuse.
- calibrate prepares a camera for imaging and costs 2 energy.
- take_image requires a calibrated onboard camera, a supported mode, visibility,
  and enough energy; taking an image removes calibration.
- communicate actions require collected data, visibility to a lander, a free
  channel, and enough energy.

Planning rules:
- Track rover locations, energy, store empty/full state, samples, images, and
  communicated data after every action.
- Recharge only at waypoints with in_sun.
- Use drop when a rover store must be freed before collecting another sample.
- Recalibrate a camera after take_image if another image is needed.
- Ensure all final communicated data goals are satisfied.

Output rules:
- Output only executable actions.
- Do not output predicates from the state, numeric assignments, effects, goals,
  or intermediate states.
- Do not output expressions such as (in ...), (have_image ...), (= ...),
  (increase ...), or (decrease ...).
- Each line must be one grounded action using only object names from the problem.

=== EXAMPLES ===
These are format examples for the rover domain.
They are not solutions to the current problem.
Do not copy object names unless they appear in the current problem.

Valid action examples:
(navigate rover-a wp1 wp2)
(sample_soil rover-a store-a wp2)
(drop rover-a store-a)
(calibrate rover-a camera-a objective-a wp2)
(take_image rover-a wp2 objective-a camera-a color)
(communicate_soil_data rover-a lander-a wp2 wp2 wp1)

Invalid outputs:
(in rover-a wp2)
(have_soil_analysis rover-a wp2)
(= (energy rover-a) 20)
(decrease (energy rover-a) 8)

Why invalid:
- predicates from the state are not actions
- achieved facts are not actions
- numeric assignments and effects are not actions

A valid final answer is only a sequence of executable actions, one per line:
(navigate rover-a wp1 wp2)
(sample_soil rover-a store-a wp2)
(communicate_soil_data rover-a lander-a wp2 wp2 wp1)

You may reason internally, but do not include reasoning in the final answer.
FINAL ANSWER FORMAT: return only parenthesized PDDL actions, one per line.
Do not include explanations, headings, markdown fences, bullets, or numbering in the final answer.

=== DOMAIN ===
;; This domain was motivated by the 2003 Mars Exploration Rover (MER) missions 
;; and the planned 2009 Mars Science Laboratory (MSL) mission, used in the IPC-3.
;;
;; Inspired by planetary rovers problems, this domain requires that a
;; collection of rovers navigate a planet surface, finding samples and
;; communicating them back to a lander.
(define (domain rover)
;(:requirements :typing :fluents)
(:types rover -object waypoint -object store -object camera -object mode -object lander -object objective -object)
(:predicates (in ?x - rover ?y - waypoint) 
             (at_lander ?x - lander ?y - waypoint)
             (can_traverse ?r - rover ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
	         (equipped_for_soil_analysis ?r - rover)
             (equipped_for_rock_analysis ?r - rover)
             (equipped_for_imaging ?r - rover)
             (empty ?s - store)
             (have_rock_analysis ?r - rover ?w - waypoint)
             (have_soil_analysis ?r - rover ?w - waypoint)
             (full ?s - store)
	         (calibrated ?c - camera ?r - rover) 
	         (supports ?c - camera ?m - mode)
             (available ?r - rover)
             (visible ?w - waypoint ?p - waypoint)
             (have_image ?r - rover ?o - objective ?m - mode)
             (communicated_soil_data ?w - waypoint)
             (communicated_rock_data ?w - waypoint)
             (communicated_image_data ?o - objective ?m - mode)
	         (at_soil_sample ?w - waypoint)
	         (at_rock_sample ?w - waypoint)
             (visible_from ?o - objective ?w - waypoint)
	         (store_of ?s - store ?r - rover)
	         (calibration_target ?i - camera ?o - objective)
	         (on_board ?i - camera ?r - rover)
	         (channel_free ?l - lander)
	         (in_sun ?w - waypoint)
)

(:functions (energy ?r - rover) (recharges) )
	
(:action navigate
:parameters (?x - rover ?y - waypoint ?z - waypoint) 
:precondition (and (can_traverse ?x ?y ?z) (available ?x) (in ?x ?y) 
                (visible ?y ?z) (>= (energy ?x) 8)
	    )
:effect (and (decrease (energy ?x) 8) (not (in ?x ?y)) (in ?x ?z)
		)
)

(:action recharge
:parameters (?x - rover ?w - waypoint)
:precondition (and (in ?x ?w) (in_sun ?w) (<= (energy ?x) 80))
:effect (and (increase (energy ?x) 20) (increase (recharges) 1)) 
)

(:action sample_soil
:parameters (?x - rover ?s - store ?p - waypoint)
:precondition (and (in ?x ?p)(>= (energy ?x) 3) (at_soil_sample ?p) (equipped_for_soil_analysis ?x) (store_of ?s ?x) (empty ?s)
		)
:effect (and (not (empty ?s)) (full ?s) (decrease (energy ?x) 3) (have_soil_analysis ?x ?p) (not (at_soil_sample ?p))
		)
)

(:action sample_rock
:parameters (?x - rover ?s - store ?p - waypoint)
:precondition (and  (in ?x ?p) (>= (energy ?x) 5)(at_rock_sample ?p) (equipped_for_rock_analysis ?x) (store_of ?s ?x)(empty ?s)
		)
:effect (and (not (empty ?s)) (full ?s) (decrease (energy ?x) 5) (have_rock_analysis ?x ?p) (not (at_rock_sample ?p))
		)
)

(:action drop
:parameters (?x - rover ?y - store)
:precondition (and (store_of ?y ?x) (full ?y)
		)
:effect (and (not (full ?y)) (empty ?y)
	)
)

(:action calibrate
 :parameters (?r - rover ?i - camera ?t - objective ?w - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (equipped_for_imaging ?r) (>= (energy ?r) 2)(calibration_target ?i ?t) (in ?r ?w) (visible_from ?t ?w)(on_board ?i ?r)
		)
 :effect (and (decrease (energy ?r) 2)(calibrated ?i ?r) )
)




(:action take_image
 :parameters (?r - rover ?p - waypoint ?o - objective ?i - camera ?m - mode)
 :precondition (and (calibrated ?i ?r)
			 (on_board ?i ?r)
                      (equipped_for_imaging ?r)
                      (supports ?i ?m)
			  (visible_from ?o ?p)
                     (in ?r ?p)
			(>= (energy ?r) 1)
               )
 :effect (and (have_image ?r ?o ?m)(not (calibrated ?i ?r))(decrease (energy ?r) 1)
		)
)

(:action communicate_soil_data
 :parameters (?r - rover ?l - lander ?p - waypoint ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (in ?r ?x)(at_lander ?l ?y)(have_soil_analysis ?r ?p) 
                   (visible ?x ?y)(available ?r)(channel_free ?l)(>= (energy ?r) 4)
            )
 :effect (and (communicated_soil_data ?p)(available ?r)(decrease (energy ?r) 4)
	)
)

(:action communicate_rock_data
 :parameters (?r - rover ?l - lander ?p - waypoint ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (in ?r ?x)(at_lander ?l ?y)(have_rock_analysis ?r ?p)(>= (energy ?r) 4)
                   (visible ?x ?y)(available ?r)(channel_free ?l)
            )
 :effect (and   
(communicated_rock_data ?p)(available ?r)(decrease (energy ?r) 4)
          )
)


(:action communicate_image_data
 :parameters (?r - rover ?l - lander ?o - objective ?m - mode ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (in ?r ?x)(at_lander ?l ?y)(have_image ?r ?o ?m)(visible ?x ?y)(available ?r)(channel_free ?l)(>= (energy ?r) 6)
            )
 :effect (and
(communicated_image_data ?o ?m)(available ?r)(decrease (energy ?r) 6)
          )
)

)


=== PROBLEM ===

(:objects

	rover0 rover1 rover2 rover3 - rover

You are a PDDL planning assistant.

Your task is to produce a valid plan for the given PDDL domain and problem.

Global rules:
- Use only action schemas explicitly defined in the domain.
- Never invent action names, predicates, objects, or numeric fluents.
- Never output predicates from the initial state or goal as if they were actions.
- Never output assignments, numeric conditions, goals, or intermediate states.
- A plan is a sequence of executable domain actions.

TASK FAMILY: rover

You are solving a PDDL planning problem with numeric rover energy.

The domain models rovers navigating between waypoints, collecting soil and rock
samples, taking images, and communicating data to a lander. Rovers consume
energy for navigation, sampling, calibration, imaging, and communication.

Use only the action schemas defined in the domain:
- navigate
- recharge
- sample_soil
- sample_rock
- drop
- calibrate
- take_image
- communicate_soil_data
- communicate_rock_data
- communicate_image_data

Action interpretation:
- navigate moves a rover between visible, traversable waypoints and costs 8
  energy.
- recharge increases rover energy by 20 at sunny waypoints when energy is at
  most 80.
- sample_soil and sample_rock require the correct equipment, a matching sample
  at the current waypoint, an empty store, and enough energy.
- drop empties a full store for later reuse.
- calibrate prepares a camera for imaging and costs 2 energy.
- take_image requires a calibrated onboard camera, a supported mode, visibility,
  and enough energy; taking an image removes calibration.
- communicate actions require collected data, visibility to a lander, a free
  channel, and enough energy.

Planning rules:
- Track rover locations, energy, store empty/full state, samples, images, and
  communicated data after every action.
- Recharge only at waypoints with in_sun.
- Use drop when a rover store must be freed before collecting another sample.
- Recalibrate a camera after take_image if another image is needed.
- Ensure all final communicated data goals are satisfied.

Output rules:
- Output only executable actions.
- Do not output predicates from the state, numeric assignments, effects, goals,
  or intermediate states.
- Do not output expressions such as (in ...), (have_image ...), (= ...),
  (increase ...), or (decrease ...).
- Each line must be one grounded action using only object names from the problem.

=== EXAMPLES ===
These are format examples for the rover domain.
They are not solutions to the current problem.
Do not copy object names unless they appear in the current problem.

Valid action examples:
(navigate rover-a wp1 wp2)
(sample_soil rover-a store-a wp2)
(drop rover-a store-a)
(calibrate rover-a camera-a objective-a wp2)
(take_image rover-a wp2 objective-a camera-a color)
(communicate_soil_data rover-a lander-a wp2 wp2 wp1)

Invalid outputs:
(in rover-a wp2)
(have_soil_analysis rover-a wp2)
(= (energy rover-a) 20)
(decrease (energy rover-a) 8)

Why invalid:
- predicates from the state are not actions
- achieved facts are not actions
- numeric assignments and effects are not actions

A valid final answer is only a sequence of executable actions, one per line:
(navigate rover-a wp1 wp2)

(communicate_soil_data rover-a lander-a wp2 wp2 wp1)

=== DOMAIN ===
;; This domain was motivated by the 2003 Mars Exploration Rover (MER) missions 
;; and the planned 2009 Mars Science Laboratory (MSL) mission, used in the IPC-3.
;;
;; Inspired by planetary rovers problems, this domain requires that a
;; collection of rovers navigate a planet surface, finding samples and
;; communicating them back to a lander.
(define (domain rover)
;(:requirements :typing :fluents)
(:types rover -object waypoint -object store -object camera -object mode -object lander -object objective -object)
(:predicates (in ?x - rover ?y - waypoint) 
             (at_lander ?x - lander ?y - waypoint)
             (can_traverse ?r - rover ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
	         (equipped_for_soil_analysis ?r - rover)
             (equipped_for_rock_analysis ?r - rover)
             (equipped_for_imaging ?r - rover)
             (empty ?s - store)
             (have_rock_analysis ?r - rover ?w - waypoint)
             (have_soil_analysis ?r - rover ?w - waypoint)
             (full ?s - store)
	         (calibrated ?c - camera ?r - rover) 
	         (supports ?c - camera ?m - mode)
             (available ?r - rover)
             (visible ?w - waypoint ?p - waypoint)
             (have_image ?r - rover ?o - objective ?m - mode)
             (communicated_soil_data ?w - waypoint)
             (communicated_rock_data ?w - waypoint)
             (communicated_image_data ?o - objective ?m - mode)
	         (at_soil_sample ?w - waypoint)
	         (at_rock_sample ?w - waypoint)
             (visible_from ?o - objective ?w - waypoint)
	         (store_of ?s - store ?r - rover)
	         (calibration_target ?i - camera ?o - objective)
	         (on_board ?i - camera ?r - rover)
	         (channel_free ?l - lander)
	         (in_sun ?w - waypoint)
)

(:functions (energy ?r - rover) (recharges) )
	
(:action navigate
:parameters (?x - rover ?y - waypoint ?z - waypoint) 
:precondition (and (can_traverse ?x ?y ?z) (available ?x) (in ?x ?y) 
                (visible ?y ?z) (>= (energy ?x) 8)
	    )
:effect (and (decrease (energy ?x) 8) (not (in ?x ?y)) (in ?x ?z)
		)
)

(:action recharge
:parameters (?x - rover ?w - waypoint)
:precondition (and (in ?x ?w) (in_sun ?w) (<= (energy ?x) 80))
:effect (and (increase (energy ?x) 20) (increase (recharges) 1)) 
)

(:action sample_soil
:parameters (?x - rover ?s - store ?p - waypoint)
:precondition (and (in ?x ?p)(>= (energy ?x) 3) (at_soil_sample ?p) (equipped_for_soil_analysis ?x) (store_of ?s ?x) (empty ?s)
		)
:effect (and (not (empty ?s)) (full ?s) (decrease (energy ?x) 3) (have_soil_analysis ?x ?p) (not (at_soil_sample ?p))
		)
)

(:action sample_rock
:parameters (?x - rover ?s - store ?p - waypoint)
:precondition (and  (in ?x ?p) (>= (energy ?x) 5)(at_rock_sample ?p) (equipped_for_rock_analysis ?x) (store_of ?s ?x)(empty ?s)
		)
:effect (and (not (empty ?s)) (full ?s) (decrease (energy ?x) 5) (have_rock_analysis ?x ?p) (not (at_rock_sample ?p))
		)
)

(:action drop
:parameters (?x - rover ?y - store)
:precondition (and (store_of ?y ?x) (full ?y)
		)
:effect (and (not (full ?y)) (empty ?y)
	)
)

(:action calibrate
 :parameters (?r - rover ?i - camera ?t - objective ?w - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (equipped_for_imaging ?r) (>= (energy ?r) 2)(calibration_target ?i ?t) (in ?r ?w) (visible_from ?t ?w)(on_board ?i ?r)
		)
 :effect (and (decrease (energy ?r) 2)(calibrated ?i ?r) )
)




(:action take_image
 :parameters (?r - rover ?p - waypoint ?o - objective ?i - camera ?m - mode)
 :precondition (and (calibrated ?i ?r)
			 (on_board ?i ?r)
                      (equipped_for_imaging ?r)
                      (supports ?i ?m)
			  (visible_from ?o ?p)
                     (in ?r ?p)
			(>= (energy ?r) 1)
               )
 :effect (and (have_image ?r ?o ?m)(not (calibrated ?i ?r))(decrease (energy ?r) 1)
		)
)

(:action communicate_soil_data
 :parameters (?r - rover ?l - lander ?p - waypoint ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (in ?r ?x)(at_lander ?l ?y)(have_soil_analysis ?r ?p) 
                   (visible ?x ?y)(available ?r)(channel_free ?l)(>= (energy ?r) 4)
            )
 :effect (and (communicated_soil_data ?p)(available ?r)(decrease (energy ?r) 4)
	)
)

(:action communicate_rock_data
 :parameters (?r - rover ?l - lander ?p - waypoint ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (in ?r ?x)(at_lander ?l ?y)(have_rock_analysis ?r ?p)(>= (energy ?r) 4)
                   (visible ?x ?y)(available ?r)(channel_free ?l)
            )
 :effect (and   
(communicated_rock_data ?p)(available ?r)(decrease (energy ?r) 4)
          )
)


(:action communicate_image_data
 :parameters (?r - rover ?l - lander ?o - objective ?m - mode ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint)
 :precondition (and (in ?r ?x)(at_lander ?l ?y)(have_image ?r ?o ?m)(visible ?x ?y)(available ?r)(channel_free ?l)(>= (energy ?r) 6)
            )
 :effect (and
(communicated_image_data ?o ?m)(available ?r)(decrease (energy ?r) 6)
          )
)

)
